daily.baseball_pulse:
MY TEAM
There is no relevant news for your roster today.

MY PROSPECTS
No prospect news today.

AROUND THE LEAGUE
Ronald Acuna Jr. was placed on the injured list as the Braves welcomed back Spencer Strider from his own IL stint. In Minnesota, Joe Ryan exited his start in the first inning with elbow soreness, adding to a rotation already dealing with injuries. Yusei Kikuchi landed on the Angels' 15-day IL following an abbreviated Wednesday start. Walker Jenkins, the Twins' top prospect and MLB Pipeline No. 11, suffered an apparent shoulder injury crashing into the center-field wall at Triple-A. The Giants called up Bryce Eldridge and Jesus Rodriguez, with Eldridge showing 70-grade power and a .333/.445/.518 slash line through 30 Triple-A games. Joe Mack was promoted by the Marlins to spell catcher Agustin Ramirez, and Anthony Volpe was reinstated from the Yankees' injured list before being optioned back to Triple-A as Jose Caballero continues his strong play at shortstop.

BASEBALL TODAY
The Braves improved to 25-10 with a dominant sweep, winning fifteen of their last eighteen games and posting their best 35-game start to a season in the Modern Era. The Royals completed their first sweep of the Mariners since 2017, while the Rays moved to 21-12 with a walk-off victory over the Giants. Mark Vientos went yard twice for the Mets in securing a series victory, and Mickey Moniak tied for the NL lead with eleven homers despite a loss for Colorado.
